source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/09_Usability/260303 사용목적 수정(CE 반영)/3. A-UEF-HNX_Usability Engineering File (Rev.2, 03032026).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering File for the Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB, documenting usability specification, risk-related use scenarios, verification, and summative evaluation approach.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by specifying usability engineering activities and records for mitigating usability-related risks for the Portable X-ray System.
keyPoints: The document states compliance with EN 62366-1:2015.
The HnX-PB variant is described as having only exterior design changes compared with HnX-P1, with no additional usability validation conducted for the variant addition.
The intended use states general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy and excludes mammography.
The usability process includes usability specification, formative and summative evaluations, risk management, validation activities, and user interface evaluation.
All identified hazard-related use scenarios are stated to be used for summative evaluation.
UI verification results for UI-01 through UI-08 are recorded as No issues and Pass.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Confidential This document is strictly private, confidential and intended solely for the use of H&abyz Co., Ltd.

entities.name: A-RMR-HNX
entities.evidence: Refer to Risk Management Report (Doc No. A-RMR-HNX)

concepts.name: Usability Engineering File
concepts.evidence: set of records and other documents that are produced by the usability engineering process

concepts.name: Usability Engineering Process
concepts.evidence: The usability engineering process involves analyzing, designing, and verifying the user interface to ensure it meets usability and safety requirements.

concepts.name: Hazard-related use scenario
concepts.evidence: use scenario that could lead to a hazardous situation or harm

concepts.name: Summative evaluation
concepts.evidence: user interface evaluation conducted at the end of the user interface development with the intent to obtain objective evidence that the user interface can be used safely

concepts.name: Formative evaluation
concepts.evidence: user interface evaluation conducted with the intent to explore user interface design strengths, weaknesses, and unanticipated use errors

concepts.name: Use error
concepts.evidence: user action or lack of user action while using the medical device that leads to a different result than that intended by the manufacturer or expected by the user

concepts.name: User Interface of Unknown Provenance
concepts.evidence: user interface or part of a user interface of a medical device previously developed for which adequate records of the usability engineering process of this standard are not available

concepts.name: Risk control as it relates to user interface design
concepts.evidence: To reduce use-related risk, the manufacturer shall use one or more of the following options, in the priority listed

evidence.claim: The document is Document No. A-UEF-HNX, Rev. No. 2, with revision date March 03, 2026.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document. No. A-UEF-HNX Rev. No. 2 Usability Engineering File Rev. date March 03, 2026

evidence.claim: The document title is Usability Engineering File.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Engineering File

evidence.claim: The product is a Portable X-ray System with model names HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Portable X-ray System Model name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB

evidence.claim: Revision 2 was made on March 03, 2026 according to the intended purpose.
evidence.evidenceText: 2 March 03, 2026 Revision according to the intended purpose

evidence.claim: The document specifies the usability engineering process for the Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This document specifies the usability engineering process for the Portable X-ray System (Model Name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The stated purpose is to ensure safety by mitigating risks related to usability problems.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose is to ensure safety by mitigating risks related to usability problems.

evidence.claim: The document states compliance with EN 62366-1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: This document complies with EN 62366-1:2015.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB variant has only exterior design changes compared to HnX-P1, while interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software are the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model(HnX-PB) has only changed the exterior design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: No additional usability validation will be conducted for addition of the HnX-PB variant model.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, no additional usability validation will be conducted for the addition of the variant model(HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The intended use is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children, replacing film-based systems, and not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The Portable X-ray System is indicated as a digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adults and children. It is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnoses for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Applicable standards and guidelines include EN 60601-1-6, EN 62366-1, IEC TR 62366-2, and EN ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6... EN 62366-1 Medical devices – Application of usability engineering... IEC TR 62366-2 Medical devices — Part 2... EN ISO 14971 Medical Devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 and HnX-PB are battery-powered portable X-ray systems intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 (and HnX-PB) is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: Clinical indications include traumatic and orthopedic conditions, respiratory and thoracic conditions, and abdominal conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is indicated for the diagnostic imaging of the following clinical conditions: Traumatic and Orthopedic Conditions... Respiratory and Thoracic Conditions... Abdominal Conditions

evidence.claim: Contraindications include pregnancy for abdominal and pelvic regions, bariatric patients for exams other than extremities, and mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant... Bariatric Patients: The device is strictly contraindicated for any examination other than the extremities... Mammography: This device is not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The target population is stated as adult patients.
evidence.evidenceText: 4.1.4 Target population • Age: Adult patients.

evidence.claim: The intended user profile includes radiographers, radiologists, and medical imaging specialists.
evidence.evidenceText: Experience · Radiographer, Radiologist, medical imaging specialists
evidence.pageTarget: Portable X-ray System Intended User Profile

evidence.claim: The device is intended for professional healthcare facilities such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments, limited to indoor controlled environments.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 (and HnX-PB) is intended for use in professional healthcare facilities, such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments. The device is limited to indoor operation within controlled environments where radiation safety protocols and distance requirements are maintained.

evidence.claim: Intended clinical benefits include support for diagnostic imaging, accessibility for patients with limited mobility, and diagnostic timeliness.
evidence.evidenceText: The intended clinical benefits of the HnX-P1(and HnX-PB) are as follows: Support for Diagnostic Imaging... Accessibility... Diagnostic Timeliness

evidence.claim: The device emits ionizing radiation during normal use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device emits ionizing radiation during normal use.

evidence.claim: The document identifies ionizing-radiation side effects per MDCG 2023-3 Rev.2 Point 10.
evidence.evidenceText: The following undesirable side-effects associated with ionizing radiation exposure have been identified and characterized per MDCG 2023-3 Rev.2 Point 10.

evidence.claim: The document warns the device is not intended for pediatric population, including children aged 2 years and above and infants under 2 years old.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not intended for use in the Pediatric population, which includes Children (patients aged 2 years and above) and Infants (patients under 2 years old).

evidence.claim: Primary operating functions include reading the user manual, safety warnings, charging the battery, checking power and LEDs, X-ray exposure using hand switch, setting exposure dose, holding the device, Bluetooth image preview, collimator use, and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: Primary Operating Functions  Familiarize yourself with the user manual...  Maintenance

evidence.claim: Safety-related functions include irradiation lamp and buzzer alerts, hand switch safeguards, immediate stop when hand switch is released, output monitoring, irradiation time detection, and automatic power cutoff for overcurrent or overload.
evidence.evidenceText: When X-rays are irradiated, the irradiation lamp lights up yellow and a buzzer sounds... The product is protected by automatically cutting off the power in case of overcurrent or overload.

evidence.claim: Known hazards and hazardous situations associated with use errors were not found in similar device user interfaces.
evidence.evidenceText: However, there were no hazards and hazardous situations associated with use errors.

evidence.claim: Foreseeable hazards include radiation exposure, malfunction, excessive radiation exposure, delayed diagnosis, injury, and unnecessary radiation exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: UR-01 Radiation exposure and malfunction may occur... UR-06 Overexposure to radiation... UR-07 Injury... UR-09 Unnecessary exposure to radiation

evidence.claim: All identified usage scenarios were used for summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All usage scenarios we identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: UI verification results UI-01 through UI-08 were recorded as No issues and Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: UI-01 No issues Pass UI-02 No issues Pass UI-03 No issues Pass UI-04 No issues Pass UI-05 No issues Pass UI-06 No issues Pass UI-07 No issues Pass UI-08 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report Doc. No. A-UVR-HNX.
evidence.evidenceText: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-UVR-HNX).

evidence.claim: User Interface of Unknown Provenance is not applicable to the Portable X-ray System HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: However, this clause is not applicable on our device, Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal indicates CE/MDR in metadata, but the source text does not explicitly identify MDR or a notified body; authorityOrJurisdiction is inferred only from EN standards and MDCG guidance mentioned in the text.

Potential conflicting intended population evidence: the scope says the device targets both adults and children, while the target population states adult patients and the warning states the device is not intended for pediatric population.
Internal section references appear inconsistent: the text refers to section 5.4 and clauses 5.6.1, 5.6.2, and 5.7.2, while the visible document sections are numbered 4.4, 4.6.1, 4.6.2, and 4.7.2.
Page numbering in extracted text is inconsistent, including Page 1 of 2 followed by Page 2 of 3 and later Page 22 of 22, suggesting preprocessing or OCR pagination artifacts.
